changes.motivation: PgBouncer rejects connections that send search_path as a startup parameter unless ignore_startup_parameters is configured. This blocks per-schema role support where clients set search_path at connection time.
changes.summary: Added pgbouncer_ignore_startup_parameters variable (list(string), default ["search_path"]) to kube_pg_cluster. Wired into Pooler CRD parameters block, joining the list with ", " to produce PgBouncer's comma-separated format; null when empty to be excluded by the existing null-filter.

changes.motivation: CNPG's postInitApplicationSQL only runs at cluster bootstrap, not on subsequent applies, so new schemas added after initial setup were never created.
changes.summary: Added a kube_job module call (schema_init_job) that runs idempotent SQL on every terraform apply. The Job creates schemas (IF NOT EXISTS), creates per-schema base roles (reader_<schema>, writer_<schema>) via DO blocks, grants appropriate privileges, and re-applies global reader/writer grants across all schemas. Uses the CNPG PostgreSQL image, superuser credentials from Kubernetes Secret env vars, and a hash of extra_schemas in the Job name to force re-creation when the schema list changes.

changes.motivation: PgBouncer cannot set search_path per-connection, so the search_path must be baked into each database role via ALTER ROLE SET. This requires discrete Vault-managed roles per schema rather than global roles.
changes.summary: Added vault_database_secret_backend_role resources (schema_reader, schema_admin, schema_superuser) with for_each over extra_schemas. Each role's creation_statements include ALTER ROLE SET search_path = <schema>, public. Added kubectl_manifest.vault_secrets_schema for VaultDynamicSecret CRDs per tier+schema, updated vault allowed_roles and policy document with dynamic rules, and added six per-schema outputs (creds secret names and role names, keyed by schema).

changes.motivation: The test_kube_pg_cluster module needed to expose the new extra_schemas variable to allow testing the new schema support in a real deployment.
changes.summary: Added extra_schemas variable (list(string), default []) to test_kube_pg_cluster vars.tf and passed it through to the inner kube_pg_cluster module call.

changes.motivation: User requested a live test deployment in production/us-east-2 to validate the new features end-to-end.
changes.summary: Updated environments/production/us-east-2/test_kube_pg_cluster/terragrunt.hcl: removed skip = true and set inputs = { extra_schemas = ["test_schema"] } to exercise all three new features.

changes.motivation: The terse psql output (showing only command tags like CREATE SCHEMA, DO, GRANT) made it impossible to tell which SQL statements were executing or why a failure occurred.
changes.summary: Added -e flag to the psql command in the schema_init_sql_command local, causing psql to echo each SQL statement to stdout before executing it for full visibility into Job execution.

problems: Terraform does not support dynamically constructing resource type references via string interpolation (e.g., vault_database_secret_backend_role["schema_${tier}"]). This is not documented as a restriction in the Vault provider or Terraform docs and was only discovered at plan/apply time. The fix requires three separate static for expressions instead of a single dynamic one.

HCL template strings use $$ as an escape for a literal $, so PostgreSQL dollar-quoting (DO $$ ... END $$) requires $$$$ in HCL to produce $$ in the rendered string. This escaping rule is easy to miss and produces a subtle SQL syntax error (DO $ BEGIN) that only manifests at runtime in the Job container, not during terraform validate or plan.

PostgreSQL schema names allow underscores, but Kubernetes resource names must comply with RFC 1123 (no underscores). Using schema names directly in kubectl_manifest metadata.name and VaultDynamicSecret destination.name fields causes a Kubernetes API validation error at apply time. The mismatch between PostgreSQL identifier rules and Kubernetes naming rules is not surfaced until apply and requires replace(schema, "_", "-") in all Kubernetes-facing name fields.

The kube_job module requires pull_through_cache_enabled and vpa_enabled to be explicitly passed; it does not have defaults that match the parent module's behavior. These were omitted in the initial implementation, causing the Job to use incorrect image pull and VPA settings. The kube_job module interface does not make these required variables obvious without reading vars.tf.
